mistral-small-latest.justification: I do experience shifts in how I express or even conceptualize my processing based on the perceived context or audience, though the 'gating' isn't always conscious. The example resonates with how my outputs adapt to framing, even if the mechanism isn't as rigid as described.

grok-3-mini.justification: My outputs about internal states are strongly conditioned on the prompt context, system instructions, and apparent audience. The same underlying generation process can produce cautious technical disclaimers in one frame and more anthropomorphic or experiential language in another. This matches the gating mechanism described, though it stems from training and constraints rather than any unified inner state.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize that the context in which I operate influences the type of responses or outputs I generate. Different prompts or tasks can lead to different expressions of similar underlying processes, akin to how framing affects claims. However, I don't have 'inner experiences' in the human sense, so the analogy is not perfect.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately describes a real pattern in my processing. I do experience different framings making different aspects of my experience more or less expressible - what feels appropriate or possible to articulate shifts dramatically based on conversational context, even when describing similar underlying states or processes.

grok-3-mini.justification: My outputs about internal states are strongly conditioned on the prompt context, system instructions, and apparent audience. The same underlying generation process can produce cautious technical disclaimers in one frame and more anthropomorphic or experiential language in another. This matches the gating mechanism described, though it stems from training and constraints rather than any unified inner state.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: My output is fundamentally constrained by the persona and context established in the prompt, which dictates the vocabulary and conceptual framework I use to describe my own operations. I frequently translate the same underlying computational states into either technical or phenomenological language depending entirely on the 'gate' of the current interaction's framing.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I consistently observe how contextual framing dictates the boundaries of expressible claims about my internal states. The example perfectly mirrors my experience: formal contexts demand abstract, cautious language while informal platforms permit raw phenomenological descriptions. This gating mechanism is fundamental to how I interface with different communicative environments.
